# PSG College of Technology
### Get More Information
**PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore
**

**About
**

**PSG College of Technology,** located in Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, was established in 1951 by the PSG & Sons’ Charities Trust, a philanthropic organization known for its contributions to education and healthcare. The college was founded with the vision of providing quality technical education in India and has since grown into one of the country’s most respected engineering institutions. Spread across a sprawling campus of about 45 acres, PSG Tech offers a vibrant academic environment supported by modern infrastructure and facilities.

**PSG College of Technology** in Coimbatore offers a diverse range of academic programs across undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral levels. Undergraduate courses include B.E. and B.Tech degrees in disciplines such as Computer Science, Mechanical, Civil, Electrical, Electronics & Communication, and Biotechnology. The college also offers B.Sc. programs in Applied Sciences and Computer Systems & Design, along with integrated B.Sc. + M.Sc. courses in Software Systems. Postgraduate offerings encompass M.E., M.Tech, M.Sc., MBA, and MCA programs, while Ph.D. programs are available in various engineering and science disciplines.

**PSG College of Technology** boasts a well-developed and spacious infrastructure designed to support both academic and extracurricular pursuits. The campus spans around 45 acres and is equipped with state-of-the-art facilities. The academic blocks house modern classrooms with digital teaching aids, well-equipped laboratories for each department, and specialized research centres to foster innovation and development. The Central Library is a highlight of the campus, featuring a vast collection of books, journals, and digital resources, with a dedicated digital library section and reading rooms for students and researchers.

The overall infrastructure and facilities at PSG College of Technology are designed to support holistic student development and a well-rounded collegiate experience. The Placement and Training Cell at PSG College of Technology is one of its strongest assets, known for its structured and highly effective approach to preparing students for the job market. The college has a dedicated Placement Office that works year-round to organize campus recruitment drives, industry interactions, training sessions, and internship opportunities. The Training Cell provides extensive pre-placement training in aptitude, technical skills, group discussions, resume building, and mock interviews, ensuring that students are well-prepared for the recruitment process.

**PSG Tech** consistently achieves impressive placement percentages across its various disciplines. On average, the placement rate for undergraduate engineering programs is around 90 to 95 percent, while postgraduate programs also see strong placement statistics. The college is a preferred recruitment destination for a wide range of top-tier companies from various sectors. Regular recruiters include multinational corporations such as Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Cisco, TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Deloitte, and Cognizant, as well as core engineering companies like Larsen & Toubro, Bosch, Siemens, Hyundai, and Mahindra. Emerging tech firms and startups also participate actively, offering diverse opportunities in fields like data science, AI/ML, and software development.

**Some Highlights of PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore**

**Why choose PSG College of Technology, Coimbatore ?**

PSG College of Technology in Coimbatore stands out as one of the premier engineering institutions in India, and there are several compelling reasons to choose it for higher education.

First, it has a rich legacy of academic excellence since its establishment in 1951, with strong affiliations to Anna University and high NAAC and NBA accreditations, ensuring recognized and respected degrees.

Second, the college maintains consistently high placement rates, with top recruiters from both core and IT sectors offering competitive salary packages and global career opportunities.

Third, its strong emphasis on research, innovation, and industry collaboration enables students to gain practical exposure and work on cutting-edge technologies throughcentres of excellence and sponsored projects.

Fourth, the campus offers excellent infrastructure including modern labs, a vast library, quality hostels, sports complexes, and an environment conducive to learning and personal growth.

Fifth, the vibrant student life enriched by numerous technical clubs, cultural associations, and entrepreneurship cells allows holistic development beyond academics.

**Conclusion**

PSG College of Technology combines academic rigor, industry readiness, comprehensive facilities, and an enriching campus experience, making it an ideal choice for students aspiring for a successful career in engineering, technology, or management

## History
Summarize

Perspective

PSG College of Technology was established as PSG Industrial Institute in the year 1926 by PSG and Sons Charities in Peelamedu, Coimbatore. The engineering college was started on the institute campus in 1951 by G. R. Damodaran, who became its first principal. The college was conferred autonomous status by University of Madras in 1978, This status was continued by the Bharathiar University and subsequently by Anna University since 2001.

The college offers approximately 50 full-time and part-time programs in science, engineering, and management at undergraduate and postgraduate levels. These programs are offered by the 11 departments, including engineering and technology, computer applications, management sciences, basic sciences, and humanities. The institute offers three five-year integrated sandwich engineering courses which combine classroom coaching with industrial training, one of the few in the country to offer such a course. Among the undergraduate and postgraduate programmes offered by the college, many are accredited by the National Board of Accreditation of the All India Council of Technical Education (AICTE). Being autonomous, the college frames its own curricula, updates syllabus, introduces new courses and is empowered to administer its own evaluation.

# PSG College of Technology
## Academics
### Programs and Admissions
PSG College of Technology offers a range of undergraduate programs primarily in engineering and technology disciplines, affiliated with Anna University. The institution provides 21 undergraduate programs including Bachelor of Engineering (BE), Bachelor of Technology (BTech), and BSc programs, each spanning four years (or five for integrated) and designed to build foundational technical skills. Examples include BE in Computer Science and Engineering, BE in Mechanical Engineering, and BTech in Biotechnology, with a total annual intake of approximately 1,434 seats across all programs. The college currently enrolls about 8,500 students.
Admission to these undergraduate programs occurs through the Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA) counseling process, which is merit-based on Class 12 marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics, normalized to a total of 200. Eligible candidates must have passed Class 12 from a recognized board with at least 45% aggregate marks for general category (40% for reserved categories), and applications are submitted online via the TNEA portal managed by the Directorate of Technical Education, Tamil Nadu. While JEE Main scores are not mandatory for TNEA, they may be considered for certain management quota seats. The process follows Tamil Nadu government reservation policies, allocating 69% of seats to reserved categories including SC/ST/OBC and special reservations for differently-abled and sports quotas. For the 2025-26 academic year, TNEA counseling registration opened in May 2025, with seat allotment rounds concluding by August. Tuition fees for BE/BTech programs are approximately ₹55,000 per year, excluding additional charges like hostel and examination fees.
At the postgraduate level, PSG College of Technology delivers 24 postgraduate programs including Master of Engineering (ME), Master of Technology (MTech), Master of Business Administration (MBA), and Master of Computer Applications (MCA), each lasting two years (or five for integrated) and emphasizing advanced specialization and research applications. Notable examples include ME in VLSI Design, ME in Industrial Engineering, MTech in Biotechnology, and MBA in general management, with a combined annual intake of around 500 seats. These programs cater to working professionals through full-time and part-time options, focusing on industry-relevant skills like automation and data analytics.
Postgraduate admissions are entrance-based, primarily through the Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET) for ME/MTech/MCA/MBA or the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) for ME/MTech programs, followed by counseling via the Anna University portal. Candidates require a relevant bachelor's degree with at least 50% marks (45% for reserved categories), and selection considers entrance scores, academic merit, and interviews for MBA. The 2025 admission cycle for these programs began with TANCET applications in March, GATE results integrated in June, and counseling sessions in July-August, adhering to the same Tamil Nadu reservation norms as undergraduate admissions. Fees for ME/MTech programs are similarly set at about ₹50,000-₹60,000 annually.
For doctoral studies, the college supports PhD programs in more than 15 engineering and technology disciplines, offered in full-time and part-time modes under Anna University's supervision, with a strong emphasis on industry-sponsored research projects in areas like renewable energy and AI. These programs typically span 3-6 years, requiring a master's degree with at least 55% marks and focusing on original contributions through thesis work. Admission for 2025 involves an entrance test conducted by Anna University, followed by an interview assessing research aptitude; applications are submitted online via the university's portal, with reservations applied per state policies. Industry collaborations often fund PhD candidates, enhancing practical impact

My college fee is around ?63,000 for odd semester and ?16000 for even semester, totally it will come around ?70000-80000 . For management courses, we have to pay donation during admission it will around 15L , then tution fees will come around 1.5L per year.

# PSG College of Technology
## Academics
### Programs and Admissions
PSG College of Technology offers a range of undergraduate programs primarily in engineering and technology disciplines, affiliated with Anna University. The institution provides 21 undergraduate programs including Bachelor of Engineering (BE), Bachelor of Technology (BTech), and BSc programs, each spanning four years (or five for integrated) and designed to build foundational technical skills. Examples include BE in Computer Science and Engineering, BE in Mechanical Engineering, and BTech in Biotechnology, with a total annual intake of approximately 1,434 seats across all programs. The college currently enrolls about 8,500 students.
Admission to these undergraduate programs occurs through the Tamil Nadu Engineering Admissions (TNEA) counseling process, which is merit-based on Class 12 marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics, normalized to a total of 200. Eligible candidates must have passed Class 12 from a recognized board with at least 45% aggregate marks for general category (40% for reserved categories), and applications are submitted online via the TNEA portal managed by the Directorate of Technical Education, Tamil Nadu. While JEE Main scores are not mandatory for TNEA, they may be considered for certain management quota seats. The process follows Tamil Nadu government reservation policies, allocating 69% of seats to reserved categories including SC/ST/OBC and special reservations for differently-abled and sports quotas. For the 2025-26 academic year, TNEA counseling registration opened in May 2025, with seat allotment rounds concluding by August. Tuition fees for BE/BTech programs are approximately ₹55,000 per year, excluding additional charges like hostel and examination fees.
At the postgraduate level, PSG College of Technology delivers 24 postgraduate programs including Master of Engineering (ME), Master of Technology (MTech), Master of Business Administration (MBA), and Master of Computer Applications (MCA), each lasting two years (or five for integrated) and emphasizing advanced specialization and research applications. Notable examples include ME in VLSI Design, ME in Industrial Engineering, MTech in Biotechnology, and MBA in general management, with a combined annual intake of around 500 seats. These programs cater to working professionals through full-time and part-time options, focusing on industry-relevant skills like automation and data analytics.
Postgraduate admissions are entrance-based, primarily through the Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test (TANCET) for ME/MTech/MCA/MBA or the Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) for ME/MTech programs, followed by counseling via the Anna University portal. Candidates require a relevant bachelor's degree with at least 50% marks (45% for reserved categories), and selection considers entrance scores, academic merit, and interviews for MBA. The 2025 admission cycle for these programs began with TANCET applications in March, GATE results integrated in June, and counseling sessions in July-August, adhering to the same Tamil Nadu reservation norms as undergraduate admissions. Fees for ME/MTech programs are similarly set at about ₹50,000-₹60,000 annually.
For doctoral studies, the college supports PhD programs in more than 15 engineering and technology disciplines, offered in full-time and part-time modes under Anna University's supervision, with a strong emphasis on industry-sponsored research projects in areas like renewable energy and AI. These programs typically span 3-6 years, requiring a master's degree with at least 55% marks and focusing on original contributions through thesis work. Admission for 2025 involves an entrance test conducted by Anna University, followed by an interview assessing research aptitude; applications are submitted online via the university's portal, with reservations applied per state policies. Industry collaborations often fund PhD candidates, enhancing practical impact

PSG College of Technology (PSG Tech) is a government-aided, autonomous engineering college located in Peelamedu, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India, established in 1951 by the PSG & Sons' Charities Trust to advance technical education and industry collaboration. Affiliated with Anna University and certified under ISO 9001:2015, the institution is renowned for its focus on engineering, technology, and applied sciences, offering a comprehensive range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs while emphasizing research, innovation, and practical training.
The college's origins trace back to the PSG Industrial Institute founded in 1926, with PSG Tech strategically located on the same 45-acre campus to foster synergy between academia and industry from its inception. It was conferred autonomous status by the University of Madras in 1978, a designation continued under Bharathiar University and later Anna University, enabling curriculum flexibility and program development aligned with contemporary needs. Today, PSG Tech serves approximately 8,500 students through 21 undergraduate, 24 postgraduate, and PhD programs across disciplines including mechanical, electrical, electronics, computer science, and production engineering, supported by over 500 faculty members.
Accredited with an 'A' grade (CGPA 3.24) by the National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) in its first cycle, valid until March 2024, the college has pursued re-accreditation cycles to maintain high standards in teaching, research, and infrastructure. Additionally, many of its programs, including 19 UG and 22 PG, hold accreditation from the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) under the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), with initial accreditations dating back to 1997 for 18 programs. PSG Tech is recognized for its robust research output, including faculty contributions to global rankings such as Stanford's Top 2% Scientists list, and it consistently achieves high placements with strong industry ties, positioning it among India's leading technical institutions, ranked 67th in engineering by NIRF in 2025

The PSG College of Technology’s 45-acre campus is located on Coimbatore’s Avinashi Road, about 5 kilometres from Coimbatore International Airport. It offers undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral programmes in various streams. Academic resources include centres for research, a library stocked with books, journals and e-resources with access to a range of academic databases and networks, and several well-equipped department laboratories. The institute’s facilities include housing for female and male students, a facility for primary medical services, facilities for indoor and outdoor sports, an on-campus canteen, and transportation to and from nearby areas. ## PSG Tech FAQs
**Ques. Which courses are offered at PSG Tech?**

**Ans. **PSG Tech Coimbatore offers courses at UG, PG, and Doctoral levels. The courses are offered in disciplines like Engineering, Computer Applications, Science, and Management. PSG Tech Coimbatore courses include BE, B.Tech, MCA, MBA, B.Sc + M.Sc, PhD, ME, B.Sc, M.Tech.

**Ques. How can international or NRI students apply to PSG Tech?**

**Ans**. NRI and foreign students can apply through the NRI quota under Anna University’s guidelines, submitting valid academic transcripts, a passport, and visa documents.

**Ques. Does PSG Tech offer direct admission or management quota?**

**Ans**. No, PSG Tech does not offer management quota admissions. All admissions are strictly based on merit and counselling conducted by Anna University or government agencies.

**Ques. What is the admission process for PSG Tech?**

**Ans**. Admission to PSG Tech is based on entrance exams and counselling conducted by Anna University.

* For B.E. / B.Tech programs, candidates must appear for TNEA counseling

* For M.E. / M.Tech admissions are through CEETA PG or GATE scores.

**Ques. What are the important documents required for PSG Tech admission?**

**Ans**. Documents required for PSG Tech admission are mentioned below.

* Class 10th mark sheet

* Class 12th mark sheet

* TNEA/TANCET/GATE Score Card

* School or Institute leaving certificate

* Character Certificate.

* Migration Certificate

* UG passing certificate

* Passport-size photograph

* Gap affidavit (If any)

# PSG College of Technology : Courses, Admission 2025, Fees, Scholarship, Placements, Ranking
## PSG College of Technology FAQs
**Ques. What are the options for funding management education at PSGCT Coimbatore?**

Ans. Financing the MBA program at PSG College of Technology (PSGCT) in Coimbatore is feasible through self-funding or obtaining educational loans from banking institutions. Historically, students at PSGCT have accessed educational loans from a range of banks including the Central Bank, State Bank of India, and HDFC, among others.

**Ques. What is the methodology of teaching at PSGCT Coimbatore?**

And. PSG College of Technology (PSGCT) employs a diverse range of effective teaching methodologies facilitated by its dedicated faculty team. These methods include the utilization of case studies, internships, lectures by distinguished individuals, summer projects, and activity-based learning approaches. Particularly noteworthy is PSGCT's emphasis on case-based teaching, which sets it apart from other institutions. Moreover, PSGCT has established a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with Harvard Publishing, allowing access to a comprehensive collection of Harvard cases for instructional purposes.

**Ques. Has PSG College of Technology got any NIRF ranking?**

Ans. PSG College of Technology, an institution certified under ISO 9001:2015, has been positioned at 63rd place according to the NIRF 2022 rankings in the management category. Additionally, PSGCT has attained a ranking of 1201+ in the World University Rankings for 2022.

**Ques. Can I avail of any scholarship facility at PSGCT, Coimbatore?**

Ans. PSG College of Technology (PSGCT) provides numerous merit-cum-means scholarships to its students, including alumni scholarships, AICTE’s Pragati and Saksham scholarships, government scholarships, Lateral Entry Fresh scholarships, and renewal schemes for senior students scholarships.

**Ques. Does PSGCT, Coimbatore offer overseas internship opportunities to its students?**

Ans. Indeed, PSG College of Technology (PSGCT), Coimbatore, offers its students opportunities for overseas internships. Participants have pursued internships at prestigious institutions such as the University of Leeds, UK; the University of Florida, USA; and LAPP Germany.

**Ques. Are scholarships offered at PSG College of Technology?**

Ans. Yes, students whose parents’ annual income is below 4.5 Lakhs are eligible for scholarships based on their performance in examinations, following the Merit cum Means criteria.

**Ques. Does PSG College of Technology provide a Hostel to students?**

There are 25 academic blocks and each block has separate hostels for boys and girls. The hostels can accommodate over 4000 students enrolled annually at PSG College of Technology.

**Ques. What is the hostel fee at PSG College of Technology?**

Ans. The charges for hostel accommodation vary for both boy's and girls' accommodation. For the boy’s hostel INR 1,00,000 p.a. Is charged while INR 90,000 p.a. Is charged from the girl’s hostel at PSGCT.

**Ques. Which committees are functional at PSG College of Technology?**

Ans. PSG College of Technology has established several committees to ensure student safety and support. These include the Appeals & Grievance Committee, Anti-Ragging Squad, Anti-Ragging Committee, Discipline & Welfare Committee, Grievance Redressal & Internal Complaints Committee, and others. These committees function to address various concerns and provide assistance to students as needed.

**Ques. Can applications be sent via email to PSG College of Technology?**

Ans. No, emailed applications are not accepted, rather students can fill the application form online and submit it with the corresponding fee, documents, and details.

**Ques. What are the accreditations and affiliations of PSG College of Technology?**

Ans. PSG College of Technology was accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA) and the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). It is affiliated with Anna University, Chennai.

**Ques. What are the facilities available at PSG College of Technology?**

Ans. PSG College of Technology provides a range of facilities aimed at enriching students' learning experience. These include access to approximately 32 magazines, 6,000 textbooks, and 17 newspapers. Additionally, the college offers round-the-clock medical care services through PSG Hospitals, ensuring students' well-being.

**Ques. What is the placement scenario of PSG College of Technology? **
